title: The Three Price Drops the White House Cited vs. The One That Matters
narrative:
**Adversarial Analysis | May 17, 2026**

White House spokesperson Kush Desai stated: "The American economy remains on a solid trajectory... March CPI showed cooling core inflation and prices of beef, dairy, eggs, and prescription drugs actually declining thanks to the President's policies." Trump predicted on May 12 that inflation would drop as soon as the Iran war ends. The public narrative: the war's economic damage is contained and temporary, with the administration crediting its own policies for cooling prices.

The selective citation tells a different story.
telemetry: Brent crude up 44% since the war began; trading at ~$105/barrel
US gasoline averaging $4.06/gallon; pre-war level was $2.98
PCE projected at 4% by year-end — double the Fed's 2% target
Dallas Fed research: war could add 0.2 to 1.8 percentage points to Q4/Q4 headline inflation in 2026
EY-Parthenon: GDP drag of 0.3 percentage points, cutting growth to 1.8% vs. 2.1% in 2025
IEA: global natural gas supplies will remain tight for two years due to the conflict
Mark Zandi (Moody's): "The damage has already been done, in part because there is no going back on oil prices"
Desai cited beef, dairy, eggs (deflating) while ignoring energy — the sector driving CPI surge
analysis:
Desai found deflation in three narrow food categories. He did not mention energy — the sector that is driving the entire CPI surge. Beef, dairy, and eggs are not meaningless, but they are not the thing that moved oil from $73 to $105 a barrel.

The Dallas Fed's own research projects the war could add 0.2 to 1.8 percentage points to headline inflation by Q4 2026. EY-Parthenon estimates GDP growth gets cut from 2.1% to 1.8%. Moody's Mark Zandi put it plainly: the damage is already done because there is no going back on oil prices in the near future.

Gas is expected to settle at $3.50 at best by year-end. Still above pre-war levels. Permanently. The food prices the White House cited are seasonally corrected short-term moves. The energy infrastructure driving $4 gas is a multi-year reality.

What the administration cited: beef down, dairy down, eggs down. What the administration did not cite: Brent crude +44%, gasoline at $4.06, CPI at 3.3% (highest since 2024), PCE tracking to 4% (double the Fed target).

Zandi's verdict: damage already done. The selective three-point rebuttal does not address the actual number driving the problem. It is a press release written around a crisis rather than a response to one.
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT]: The White House found deflation in beef, dairy, and eggs. It did not mention Brent crude up 44%, gas at $4.06, or CPI at a two-year high. The three price drops the administration cited are real. The one price that matters most is the one they did not mention.
